went_well: Shipped three major versions in one session with zero regressions. Pete's SQLite insight was correct — it eliminated the need for hand-rolled inverted indexes, trigram code, file locking shims, and JSONL dedup-on-read. The plan→execute flow was smooth: ULTRATHINK planning, then clean execution. Test suite went from 338 to 392 tests. Production migration worked cleanly.
missed: Should have used Aleph for Null codebase navigation from the start — Pete had to correct me. The MCP server running this session was the old version the entire time, so all null_observe calls went through JSONL code, not the new SQLite backend. Should have flagged this earlier.
do_differently: Use Aleph in every repo that has .aleph/ set up, not just the Aleph repo. Check early in session whether MCP server version matches the code being worked on. When doing major rewrites, consider whether the running MCP server needs a restart mid-session.
